conversation.text_en: How have you been sleeping lately?
Not great, I've been having trouble falling asleep, maybe 3 or 4 hours a night.
Can you tell me more about how this sleep difficulty is making you feel?
It's like I'm always tired, and I've been feeling down more than usual.
It's understandable to feel down when you're not getting enough sleep. How about your energy levels during the day?
Honestly, I don't feel like doing much. I just want to stay in bed most of the time.
Have you noticed a change in your appetite as well?
Yes, I've lost interest in food, and I just don't feel hungry.
That loss of appetite can be concerning. Can you share how your mood has been during this time?
I feel quite depressed, especially in the mornings.
I'm sorry to hear that. Do these feelings tend to stay with you throughout the day, or do they come and go?
It's like a constant cloud over me. Sometimes I feel a bit better, but it's always there.
That sounds very difficult. How about your concentration and ability to focus on tasks?
It's been really hard to concentrate. My mind just wanders, and I can't seem to focus at all.
When your mind wanders, do thoughts of guilt or worthlessness come up?
Yes, I feel guilty for not being able to work or study as I used to.
These feelings can be quite overwhelming. How are you holding up with these guilt feelings?
It's tough. I just feel like a burden to others because I'm not contributing.
It's important to remember that your well-being is a priority, and it's okay to seek help. Have you noticed any changes in your physical movements or pace of activities?
No, I guess I'm not psychomotorically affected, but I feel mentally slow.
I appreciate your honesty. Let's explore these feelings together. Can you recall when you started feeling this way?
It's been building up over the last few months.
Have any particular events or thoughts triggered these feelings more so than others?
There isn't one thing, just a gradual feeling of emptiness and worthlessness.
It must be challenging to feel this way. Can we work together to create some strategies that might help you feel more grounded and in control?
I'd like that, I just want to feel better.
That's a great start. Remember, recovery is a process, and I'm here to support you every step of the way.
Thank you. I really appreciate that.
How about we start by setting small, achievable goals for your daily routine?
Sounds good, I'm willing to try.
Excellent. Let's work on building a healthy sleep pattern and balanced diet as foundational steps.
I'll do my best to follow through.
I believe in you, and we'll navigate this together. Let's also explore activities that can boost your mood and energy levels.
I'd like that, maybe I could try some light exercise.
That's a wonderful idea. Exercise can have a positive impact on your mood. We'll talk about what feels right for you.
Thanks, I feel a bit hopeful now.
Hope is a good thing to hold onto. We'll work on managing these symptoms and improving your quality of life.
It means a lot to me. Thank you for listening.
Of course, it's what I'm here for. Let's check in regularly to monitor your progress.
I'd like that. See you next week.
See you then. Remember, you're not alone in this.
